steps.expected: Worldview tab active with empty scene strip
steps.observed: Worldview tab active, 'Create a new scene to get started' message displayed

steps.expected: Scene chip created, 'Create a 3D World' CTA visible
steps.observed: Scene 1 chip created and active, CTA displayed

steps.expected: World creator panel opens with prompt textarea and Generate button
steps.observed: World creator panel opened with text/image toggle, textarea, model selector, and disabled Generate World button

steps.action: Enter prompt 'A mystical forest with glowing mushrooms and a crystal stream'
steps.observed: Generate World button enabled after text entered

steps.action: Clear console errors, then click 'Generate World'
steps.expected: API call fails gracefully, toast.error shows descriptive message, no unhandled promise rejections
steps.observed: Toast notification appeared: 'Failed to send a request to the Edge Function'. No unhandled promise rejections in console. Console showed expected CORS/401 network errors from the edge function (API key not configured), but all were handled gracefully.

evidence.consoleErrors: No unhandled promise rejections. Expected network errors: CORS block on worldlabs-proxy (401 status, no API key). React validateDOMNesting warning (button nested in button — pre-existing). All API failures caught and displayed as toast notifications.
evidence.network: POST to worldlabs-proxy edge function -> CORS blocked (no API key configured)

assertions.title: Inline error state for failed world generation

steps.action: After world generation fails (from VAL-POLISH-001 flow)
steps.expected: Inline error state appears in world card area with error message and retry option
steps.observed: Inline error card appeared showing: 'World generation failed' title (in red/amber text), 'Failed to send a request to the Edge Function' descriptive message, and a 'Retry' button with refresh icon

evidence.consoleErrors: none (checked after generation failure — no unhandled errors)
evidence.network: N/A — testing inline UI error state display

assertions.title: Multi-scene state isolation

steps.action: Scene 1 already has failed world generation error state from previous tests
steps.observed: Scene 1 shows inline error card with 'World generation failed' — correct

steps.action: Click '+ New Scene' to create Scene 2
steps.expected: Scene 2 created and activated, showing empty 'Create a 3D World' CTA
steps.observed: Scene 2 created and activated, but STILL shows 'World generation failed' error card from Scene 1 — state leakage

steps.action: Click Scene 1 chip to switch back
Click Scene 2 chip to switch to it
steps.expected: Scene 2 shows empty 'Create a 3D World' CTA (no error state)
steps.observed: Scene 2 still shows 'World generation failed' error card — same state as Scene 1. State is NOT isolated.

evidence.consoleErrors: N/A — testing state isolation, not error handling

assertions.issues: State isolation bug: generationError is stored as component-level React useState (WorldviewSection.tsx line 669) rather than per-scene in the Zustand store. When world generation fails in Scene A, the error persists globally and leaks into Scene B (and all other scenes). Scene B should show the empty 'Create a 3D World' CTA but instead shows Scene A's error state. Fix: either move generationError into per-scene state in the Zustand store, or reset generationError when activeSceneId changes.

frictions.description: Initial page load shows a splash screen ('INITIALIZING MOG STUDIO...') with an 'Edge Function returned a non-2xx status code' toast during initialization — requires waiting 3-5 seconds before the Worldview UI is accessible
frictions.resolution: Used agent-browser wait 5000 after initial load to allow initialization to complete

frictions.description: React validateDOMNesting warning in console: button nested inside button in SceneChip component (WorldviewSection.tsx:61). Pre-existing issue, not related to error handling assertions.
frictions.resolution: Not in scope — noted as pre-existing DOM nesting issue

summary: Tested 3 assertions: 2 passed (VAL-POLISH-001, VAL-POLISH-002), 1 failed (VAL-CROSS-006: generationError state leaks across scenes because it is tracked as component-level useState rather than per-scene Zustand state)
